input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="My App">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings: single page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to show them how to set up AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with:
1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage (navigation target from lists)

I'll create a complete example showing the XAML structure and the code-behind with route registration.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="My App">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings: single page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml** — Defines the flyout structure:
- `FlyoutItem` for **Dashboard** (single `ShellContent`)
- `FlyoutItem` for **Products** with a `Tab` containing two `ShellContent` entries ("Active" / "Archived") — these render as bottom tabs
- `FlyoutItem` for **Settings**

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a route so you can navigate to it from either product list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    ["Product"] = selectedProduct
});
```

Key points:
- Pages in the visual hierarchy get routes automatically from their `Route` property.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is only needed for pages **not** in the shell tree (like detail pages).
- The `Tab` wrapper inside the Products `FlyoutItem` is what gives you the two sub-tabs.
